articles.title: SC Settles issue of GST on Long-Term Lease of Immovable Properties
articles.summary: The Supreme Court upheld the Bombay High Court's ruling that assignment of long-term leasehold rights in immovable property is not a taxable supply under the GST law. The decision confirms that such transfers are not liable to GST due to the absence of a business nexus and their character as transfers of immovable property rights. articles.title: PFRDA Allows Government Entities to Use PoP Services for ₹500 Annual Fee
articles.summary: The Pension Fund Regulatory and Development Authority (PFRDA) has allowed government entities to use Point of Presence (PoP) services with a flat annual fee of ₹500 per subscriber. This decision aims to simplify the process for government entities to provide pension services to their employees. The move is expected to benefit government employees by providing them with a convenient and cost-effective option for pension services.
articles.raw_summary: PFRDA has permitted Government Entities to continue availing Point of Presence services despite the earlier requirement for direct CRA integration. articles.title: India to allow import of 3.78 lakh UK cars at concessional duty in first 15 years of trade pact
articles.summary: India will allow imports of up to 3.78 lakh conventional-engine passenger cars from the UK at concessional duties under a new trade pact starting July 15. Import duties will fall from 110% to 10% with quotas, while Indian electric and hybrid cars will gain duty-free access to the UK market from the sixth year.

articles.title: Rupee’s oil relief capped by RBI's FX book, interest payment hedges, bankers say
articles.summary: The Indian rupee's upward surge encounters some obstacles. As the Reserve Bank of India gradually dissolves a hefty foreign exchange forward book, banks are also preparing for interest payments on foreign currency holdings. These maneuvers are likely to soak up dollar inflows, which could limit the rupee's rise. Analysts predict a cautious outlook for the currency's future gains.

articles.title: El Nino fear pushes farmers in Srikakulam and Vizianagaram towards water-saving paddy methods
articles.summary: Farmers in Andhra Pradesh's Srikakulam and Vizianagaram districts are adopting water-saving rice farming techniques to mitigate the effects of potential El Nino. This shift aims to reduce water usage and labor costs. The Dr Reddy's Foundation is promoting these changes to ensure sustainable agriculture in the region.
articles.raw_summary: Farmers in Andhra Pradesh's Srikakulam and Vizianagaram districts are shifting to water-saving rice farming techniques. Facing potential El Nino impacts, cultivators are embracing Direct Seeded Rice and Alternate Wetting and Drying. These methods significantly reduce water usage and labor costs. The Dr Reddy's Foundation is actively promoting these changes, alongside soil health improvements, to ensure sustainable agriculture in the region.

articles.title: Govt ratifies 8.25% EPF interest rate for FY'26, to be credited this month
articles.summary: The government has approved an 8.25% interest rate on Employees' Provident Fund (EPF) deposits for FY26, clearing the proposal recommended by the Employees' Provident Fund Organisation's (EPFO) Central Board of Trustees in March. The interest is expected to be credited to the accounts of over seven crore EPF subscribers later this month.

articles.title: Finding Of Forgery Cannot Be Sustained Merely On Signatory's Denial, Without Expert Opinion: Uttarakhand High Court
articles.summary: The Uttarakhand High Court has held that a finding of forgery cannot be sustained merely on the basis of a signatory's denial of signatures without any objective verification or expert opinion. The Court observed that a finding of forgery carries serious civil consequences as well as criminal implications and therefore cannot be based on assumptions or unilateral assertions alone. Justice Pankaj Purohit was hearing a writ petition filed by a fair price shop dealer challenging a Gram Sabha resolu

articles.title: NSE flags regulatory, tech, AI, derivatives concentration risks in IPO papers
articles.summary: The National Stock Exchange raises a red flag regarding substantial financial and operational threats. Emerging regulatory frameworks, tech breakdowns, cyber threats, and the rise of AI present notable concerns. The NSE's dependency on derivatives revenue leaves it exposed. Recent adjustments from Sebi have already dampened trading volumes, and stricter regulations could further constrict both activity and profit margins.

Recent adjustments from Sebi have already dampened trading volumes, and stricter regulations could further constrict both activity and profit margins.

articles.title: Tamil Nadu finally admits its biggest debt problem. But can it afford the solution?
articles.summary: Tamil Nadu's latest fiscal White Paper identifies the power sector as the state's biggest source of financial risk, with electricity utilities carrying debt of ₹2.47 lakh crore and accumulated losses of ₹1.82 lakh crore. While recent assessments suggest the financial health of the state's distribution utility has improved, the key question is whether the turnaround is sustainable—or whether the burden has simply shifted from TNEB's balance sheet to the state's own finances.

articles.title: World Bank, India discuss framework to assess land governance reforms across states

articles.summary: The Bombay High Court has held that an heir of a deceased tenant is not required to have been residing with the tenant at the time of death to inherit tenancy under Section 5(11)(c)(i) of the Bombay Rent Act, 1947. articles.title: Telegram ban: HC asks government to present proof that justifies blocking

The court seeks supporting documents for the blocking order, while Telegram faces restrictions until June 30, with the app delisted from app stores.

articles.title: RBI not in favour of offshore settlement for government ​securities, sources say
articles.summary: The RBI is steering clear of offshore settlement for government securities, preferring foreign investors to trade directly on the domestic NDS-OM platform. Despite recent tax incentives, the RBI aims to consolidate liquidity locally for better price discovery and ease of trading, ensuring a unified market.
articles.raw_summary: The RBI is steering clear of offshore settlement for government securities, preferring foreign investors to trade directly on the domestic NDS-OM platform. Despite recent tax incentives, the RBI aims to consolidate liquidity locally for better price discovery and ease of trading, ensuring a unified market.

articles.title: CAG asks states to disclose off-budget borrowings in A/Cs
articles.summary: India's Comptroller and Auditor General, K Sanjay Murthy, urged state governments to fully disclose their off-budget borrowings. He highlighted that these liabilities have reached ₹90.51 lakh crore. States are advised to reduce these debts over time. Accurate reporting is crucial for understanding the true scale of state finances and ensuring fiscal transparency. This will aid informed decision-making by policymakers.
articles.raw_summary: India's Comptroller and Auditor General, K Sanjay Murthy, urged state governments to fully disclose their off-budget borrowings. He highlighted that these liabilities have reached ₹90.51 lakh crore. States are advised to reduce these debts over time. Accurate reporting is crucial for understanding the true scale of state finances and ensuring fiscal transparency. This will aid informed decision-making by policymakers.

articles.title: Finmin to engage with states over looming El Nino fallout
articles.summary: The Finance Ministry will soon meet with states and central ministries to assess the economic and fiscal impact of El Nino. This review will focus on potential stress on agriculture, food inflation, and increased subsidy outgo. The government is preparing relief packages for vulnerable districts. This proactive approach aims to ensure adequate supplies and mitigate inflation spikes.

This review will focus on potential stress on agriculture, food inflation, and increased subsidy outgo. The government is preparing relief packages for vulnerable districts. articles.title: Real test of AI regulations: Reducing pendency of court cases
articles.summary: The Supreme Court has released a draft of AI Regulations, which goes beyond existing precedents from other countries. The draft aims to address critical areas related to AI. The court's move is significant as it sets the stage for further discussions and potential policy changes.
articles.raw_summary: The Supreme Court’s draft AI Regulations go well beyond existing precedents from other countries. But a few critical areas still need addressing.

articles.title: With Iran-US peace deal in sight, IEA projects oil supply surplus in 2027
articles.summary: The International Energy Agency (IEA) projects a global oil supply surplus in 2027, following a disruption in West Asia. This may impact India's energy policy. The IEA expects a 3.9 million barrels per day (bpd) decrease in oil supply this year, followed by an 8 million bpd rebound next year.
articles.raw_summary: Global crude oil supply is set to fall by 3.9 million barrels per day (bpd) to 102.4 million bpd this year amid trade disruptions due to the West Asia war, before rebounding by 8 million bpd next year, the International Energy Agency (IEA) said on Wednesday.

articles.title: Crude price fall may not last as nations rush to rebuild reserves
articles.summary: Global crude oil prices may not plummet due to a supply shortfall, despite a potential US-Iran peace deal. This could have implications for India's energy policy. The article highlights the complex factors influencing global oil prices.
articles.raw_summary: Even with a US-Iran peace deal on the horizon, global crude oil prices are not expected to plummet due to a significant supply shortfall. nbsp;

articles.title: States' liabilities rise to  ₹90.5 tn in FY25, many breach fiscal gap limit: CAG
articles.summary: As of 31 March 2025, the total liabilities of states stood at ₹90.51 trillion, while outstanding public debt reached ₹75.52 trillion. This reflects growing borrowing requirements across states, with public debt more than tripling over the past decade.
articles.raw_summary: As of 31 March 2025, total liabilities of states stood at ₹90.51 trillion, while outstanding public debt reached ₹75.52 trillion. Public debt has more than tripled over the past decade, rising from ₹23.92 trillion in 2015-16, reflecting growing borrowing requirements across states.

articles.title: Shipping lines stay cautious even after US-Iran peace deal, await security clarity before resuming Gulf operations
articles.summary: Shipping lines are cautious about resuming operations in the Gulf due to ongoing maritime conflict. Major lines like Maersk and Mitsui O.S.K. are waiting for security assurances before resuming operations. The conflict has led to increased costs for shipping companies.
articles.raw_summary: The US-Iran peace agreement raises hopes for smoother shipping in the Gulf, yet major lines Maersk and Mitsui O.S.K. remain hesitant. The industry awaits essential security assurances before resuming operations as costs surge due to the ongoing maritime conflict.

articles.title: Oil continues to trade subdued; market awaits peace deal details, reopening of Strait of Hormuz
articles.summary: Crude oil prices remain low due to a US-Iran peace framework. The Strait of Hormuz reopening uncertainty keeps markets cautious. This may impact India's energy sector and trade policies.
articles.raw_summary: Crude prices hovered near three-month lows after the US and Iran agreed on a peace framework, though uncertainty over the Strait of Hormuz reopening kept markets cautious.

articles.title: Government raises export duties on diesel, ATF

articles.title: India’s unemployment rate hits 11-month high as rural joblessness rises in May

Overall electronics exports for the first two months of 2026-27 climbed by 24.4%.

articles.title: Tata says India pollution board drops scrutiny of Apple iPhone parts plant
articles.summary: Tata Electronics, an Apple supplier, has cleared pollution concerns at its iPhone plant in Tamil Nadu. The state pollution control board dropped its investigation after Tata addressed issues regarding wastewater discharge. Farmers had complained about contamination of agricultural lands. Tata confirmed its facility's water samples showed no contamination. This development is significant for Apple's production diversification efforts in India.

articles.summary: Leading Indian fintechs Groww, Zerodha, Angel One, and Upstox have secured licenses from IFSCA to offer international equity investing to Indian retail traders. Groww and Upstox obtained Global Access Provider (GAP) licenses, while Zerodha and Angel One became broker-dealers, enabling direct or indirect access to US stock markets.

articles.title: BigBasket CEO Hari Menon steps down; Amazon veteran Amit Nanda takes over

articles.title: Govt readies farm safety net with banks, insurers as El Nino looms
articles.summary: The Indian government is taking steps to protect rural families from the effects of El Nino on farming. This includes improving loan access, expanding crop insurance, and speeding up claim payouts. Technology like satellite data will be used to support these efforts.

articles.title: RBI temporarily withdraws rate caps on FCNR(B), NRE deposits till Sept 30
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India has removed interest rate caps on specific non-resident deposits. This move grants banks more freedom to attract foreign funds. The changes are effective immediately and will continue until September 2026. Banks can now offer higher rates on FCNR(B) and NRE deposits. This aims to boost long-term liability mobilisation and improve asset-liability management for financial institutions.

articles.title: UK-India free trade deal to kick in on July 15, cutting tariffs by over $480 mn in year one
articles.summary: The United Kingdom and India will implement their Free Trade Agreement on July 15. This landmark deal, signed last year, promises to boost trade significantly. Businesses have 28 days to prepare for the changes. Tariffs on many UK exports to India will be reduced, including Scotch whisky and automotive products. India will also eliminate tariffs on most UK imports.

articles.title: Maharashtra advises farmers to do fodder planning to avoid a potential shortage due to El Nino

This proactive approach aims to prevent a drop in milk production and ensure livestock health during potential feed scarcity from February to June 2027.

Services sector companies experienced a moderation in operating profit margins.

articles.summary: States' spending surged 131 percent from 2015-16 to 2024-25. Welfare and development drove this growth. Revenue expenditure remained the largest budget component. Budgetary spending averaged between 15.78 and 17.49 percent of GSDP.

articles.summary: India's government warehouses hold record rice stocks, up 15% from last year. Wheat inventories are at a five-year peak. These abundant reserves will support increased rice exports. India is well-positioned for both exports and domestic price stability.

articles.title: El Nino to hit 9-10 states severely; Chouhan calls for district-level contingency plans for Kharif
articles.summary: Agriculture Minister Shivraj Singh Chouhan has directed district officials and agricultural agencies in 9-10 states to prepare contingency plans for the Kharif season due to expected El Nino impacts. articles.title: Global commodity prices likely to remain elevated amid easing West Asia tensions; India's WPI seen at 7-8% in FY27: Report
articles.summary: Global commodity prices will stay high in coming months. Geopolitical tensions in West Asia and bad weather affecting crops are the main reasons. Supply chains will take time to recover even after conflicts end. This will impact energy, metals, fertilizers, and food prices. India's inflation is expected to average 7-8 percent in FY27.

articles.title: Cooling crude may ease inflation heat, give RBI room to stay on pause despite WPI surge: YES Bank
articles.summary: India's wholesale inflation is high, driven by fuel and manufacturing costs. However, falling global crude oil prices and a stable rupee offer potential relief. This could allow the Reserve Bank of India to postpone interest rate increases. The central bank is monitoring weather-related food inflation risks. A new Producer Price Index framework is also being introduced.

articles.title: Indian soybean acreage expected to rise on higher prices, weak monsoon outlook
articles.summary: Indian farmers are planting more soybeans this year. Higher prices for soybeans are making them more profitable than corn. Forecasts of less rain also favor soybeans, which need less water than sugarcane and corn. This shift could boost India's soybean production and reduce the need for imported edible oils. Farmers are anticipating a good season for the oilseed crop.

articles.title: Fiscal stress deepens across states as all 28 post deficits in FY25: CAG report
articles.summary: India's states continued to face mounting fiscal pressures in 2024-25, with all 28 states reporting fiscal deficits and their combined liabilities rising to Rs 90.51 lakh crore as of March 31, 2025, according to the Comptroller and Auditor General's (CAG) report State Finances 2024-25 released on Monday.

articles.title: India's export outlook is challenging for FY27, but worst is over: Elara Securities
articles.summary: India's export outlook remains challenging in FY27 amid moderating global demand and elevated electronics imports, but the worst phase appears to be behind as a weaker rupee, resilient services exports and strong remittance inflows are likely to cushion external sector pressures, according to a report by Elara Securities.

articles.title: India eyes biofertilisers after Mideast war stoked supply fears
articles.summary: Under a shed in north India, women scoop cow dung, lumps of unrefined sugar and flour to produce biofertiliser -- part of a growing effort to tackle anxieties over the supply of chemical fertilisers. India is among the world's largest consumers of chemical fertilisers, using roughly 63 million tonnes annually.

articles.title: Rupee posts longest winning run in a year on exporter flows, bank dollar sales
articles.summary: The Indian rupee strengthened against the U.S. dollar for the fifth day straight, marking its longest rally in a year. Lenders and exporters aggressively reduced their dollar holdings. The rupee closed higher, recovering from an initial dip. This upward movement was driven by fixing-related selling and strong exporter support. Declining crude oil prices also aided the rupee's rise.

articles.title: SBI to raise Rs 60,000 cr in FY27 via bonds
articles.summary: State Bank of India's board has approved raising up to Rs 60,000 crore in the current fiscal year through debt instruments. These funds will be raised via long-term bonds, Basel III-compliant Additional Tier 1, and Tier 2 Bonds. The issuance will be through public offer or private placement to Indian and overseas investors during FY27.

articles.title: Rupee hits six-week high on offshore, exporter flows
articles.summary: The Indian Rupee saw a remarkable resurgence on Thursday, hitting a six-week high after a period of weakness influenced by the Federal Reserve's strict monetary stance. This turnaround was fueled by increased dollar selling from foreign markets and robust exporter contributions. Furthermore, the recent downturn in oil prices offered additional support.

articles.title: Central banks around the globe expect gold reserves to increase
articles.summary: Central banks around the world, including those in emerging markets, plan to increase their gold reserves. articles.title: India to remain fastest-growing major economy at 6.6% in FY27, down from 7.2% last year: World Bank
articles.summary: India is expected to remain the world’s fastest-growing major economy, with the World Bank projecting GDP growth of 6.6% in FY27, down from 7.7% in FY26 due to higher energy prices and input costs affecting private demand. The report said domestic consumption remains resilient, particularly in rural areas, while GST rate cuts could support spending. Growth is forecast to recover to 7.2% in FY28, aided by stronger domestic demand, export growth, lower US tariffs and free trade agreements.

articles.title: The secret to keeping a family business alive across generations

This historical event may have implications for how India's past is perceived and understood.
